title: KDB Compile Result (page-intents model)
description: Aggregate LLM output across all sources in a compile run. The LLM emits semantic intent only: slugs, titles, bodies, logical links. Python owns paths, frontmatter, timestamps, versions, incoming-link reconciliation. See docs/CODEBASE_OVERVIEW.md D8 and KDB/CLAUDE.md.

run_id.description: Stamped by Python (run_context.py). LLM does not emit run_ids.

log_entries.description: LLM-emitted log items (contradictions, ambiguities, low-confidence decisions). Python appends to wiki/log.md.

slug.description: Lowercase ASCII kebab-case identifier. No paths, no .md suffixes, no directory separators.

sourceId.description: Canonical relative path of a raw source (set by Python; the LLM echoes back the one it was given).

pageType.description: Only LLM-authored page types. Index and log are Python-authored and not emittable by the LLM.

pageIntent.description: One page the LLM wants to create or replace. Full-body replacement model (D18).

body.description: Full markdown body, no frontmatter. Python prepends frontmatter during apply.

supports_page_existence.description: source_ids that justify this page existing. Must reference real sources (current or manifest-known).

outgoing_links.description: Slugs this page's body links to. Must appear in body as [[slug]]. Python reconciles incoming_links_known from these.

pages.description: Every page the LLM wants to create or replace on behalf of this source (summary + concepts + articles).
